title: Bionano Laboratories Announces Publication of the Largest Study To-Date Evaluating Clinical Utility of Optical Genome Mapping Across Multiple Hematologic Malignancies
text: Bionano Laboratories published findings from the largest single-institution evaluation of optical genome mapping (OGM) clinical utility in blood cancers, analyzing 519 bone marrow and peripheral blood samples across nine hematological malignancy types. OGM detected somatic aberrations missed by standard cytogenetic workup in 58% (303 of 519) cases. In 15% of cases (75 of 519), OGM identified additional clinically significant Tier 1 variants with diagnostic, prognostic, or therapeutic relevance. Detection rates varied by disease: 52% in T-ALL, 0% in MPN. Dr. Alka Chaubey, Chief Medical Officer, stated that OGM uncovered clinically actionable Tier 1 variants in 15% of cases that conventional methods like karyotyping missed, potentially impacting patient diagnosis and treatment decisions. The research appears in a special cancer biomarkers issue and demonstrates OGM's potential as a complementary tool to enhance standard cytogenetic workflows in hematologic malignancy diagnosis.
